description: Engineering patterns accumulated from ForgeOS builds

patterns.title: Supabase subscriptions table — billing state pattern
patterns.pattern: Track subscription state in a dedicated `subscriptions` table with columns: user_id (FK → auth.users), plan (text), status (active | cancelled | past_due | trialing), current_period_end (timestamptz), and the payment-provider subscription ID (lemon_subscription_id or stripe_subscription_id). Use a Postgres trigger to sync `profiles.plan` whenever status changes. Never compute plan from invoice history — the status column is the single source of truth.
patterns.when_to_use: Any SaaS that gates features behind a paid plan. Required when using Lemon Squeezy or Stripe webhook-driven billing with Supabase Auth.

patterns.title: PDF generation — DejaVuSans required for ₹ symbol
patterns.pattern: Standard PDF libraries (ReportLab, fpdf2, WeasyPrint) ship without a font that covers the Indian Rupee sign U+20B9 (₹). The glyph renders as a box or raises a UnicodeEncodeError unless DejaVuSans is explicitly registered. Bundle DejaVuSans.ttf (Apache 2.0, safe to redistribute) in the project's assets and register it before any PDF draw call. Use this font as the default body font for all Indian-locale invoices.
patterns.when_to_use: Any PDF-generating feature (invoices, contracts, receipts) that displays INR amounts in Indian-locale projects.
patterns.example:
# fpdf2 example — works identically for ReportLab and WeasyPrint
from fpdf import FPDF
import pathlib

FONT_DIR = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / 'assets' / 'fonts'

class InvoicePDF(FPDF):
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        # Register DejaVuSans before any add_page() call
        self.add_font('DejaVu', '', str(FONT_DIR / 'DejaVuSans.ttf'), uni=True)
        self.add_font('DejaVu', 'B', str(FONT_DIR / 'DejaVuSans-Bold.ttf'), uni=True)

    def amount_cell(self, amount: float) -> None:
        self.set_font('DejaVu', '', 12)
        # ₹ renders correctly with DejaVuSans
        self.cell(0, 10, f'\u20b9{amount:,.2f}', ln=True)

patterns.title: Auth — session email must come from token, never hardcoded
patterns.pattern: Never hardcode or cache a user's email in application code for session-dependent operations (payment webhooks, email dispatch, audit logs). Always resolve the current user's email from the live JWT token via `auth.get_user(token)` or `request.state.user.email` populated by the auth middleware. Hardcoded emails survive code review but fail silently in production when a user changes their email or when running integration tests with seeded data.
patterns.when_to_use: Any endpoint or background task that needs the current user's identity: webhooks, invoice generation, email notifications, audit trails.
patterns.example:
# FastAPI dependency — resolves user from Bearer token every request
from fastapi import Depends, HTTPException, status
from fastapi.security import HTTPBearer, HTTPAuthorizationCredentials
from supabase import create_client

bearer = HTTPBearer()

async def current_user(
    creds: HTTPAuthorizationCredentials = Depends(bearer)
) -> dict:
    supabase = create_client(SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_ANON_KEY)
    response = supabase.auth.get_user(creds.credentials)
    if not response.user:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED)
    # email is always fresh from the token — never hardcode it
    return {"id": response.user.id, "email": response.user.email}

@router.post('/invoices')
async def create_invoice(user: dict = Depends(current_user)):
    # Use user['email'] — resolved from live JWT, never cached or hardcoded
    send_invoice_email(to=user['email'], ...)
